title: Partial sums intro
description: Solve some problems related to sequence, series, and their partial sums.
topic_path: Defining convergent and divergent infinite series

assessment_items.question_content:
The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=\frac{n+1}{n+10}$.

**Write a rule for ${{a}_{n}}$.**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
An individual term of a series is the difference between two successive partial sums.

In particular, $\displaystyle{{a}_{n}}={{S}_{n}}-{{S}_{n-1}}\,$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=\frac{n+1}{2n+5}$.

**Write a rule for ${{a}_{n}}$.**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
An individual term of a series is the difference between two successive partial sums.

In particular, $\displaystyle{{a}_{n}}={{S}_{n}}-{{S}_{n-1}}\,$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=\frac{3n}{3n+1}$.

**Write a rule for ${{a}_{n}}$.**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
An individual term of a series is the difference between two successive partial sums.

In particular, $\displaystyle{{a}_{n}}={{S}_{n}}-{{S}_{n-1}}\,$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=2+\frac{3n+1}{4n}$.

**Write a rule for ${{a}_{n}}$.**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
An individual term of a series is the difference between two successive partial sums.

In particular, $\displaystyle{{a}_{n}}={{S}_{n}}-{{S}_{n-1}}\,$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=\frac{n-1}{n+1}$.

**Write a rule for ${{a}_{n}}$.**

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
The $~n\text{th}~$ term of the series is the difference between successive partial sums. 

That is, $\displaystyle{{a}_{n}}={{S}_{n}}-{{S}_{n-1}}\,$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=\frac{n^2-3}{n^3+4}$.
  
$\displaystyle\sum\limits_{n=1}^{6}{{{a}_{n}}}=$ [[☃ input-number 1]]

The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=6-\frac{3}{{{n}^{2}}}$.

$\displaystyle\sum\limits_{n=1}^{9}{{{a}_{n}}}=$ [[☃ input-number 1]]

The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=2+\frac{3n}{n+7}$.

$\displaystyle\sum\limits_{n=1}^{8}{{{a}_{n}}}=$ [[☃ input-number 1]]

The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=\frac{8n}{n+5}$.

$\displaystyle\sum\limits_{n=1}^{11}{{{a}_{n}}}=$ [[☃ input-number 1]]

The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=\frac{n-1}{n+1}$.

$\displaystyle\sum\limits_{n=1}^{6}{{{a}_{n}}}=$ [[☃ input-number 1]]

The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=\frac{n+1}{n^2+1}$.

$\displaystyle\sum\limits_{n=1}^{5}{{{a}_{n}}}=$ [[☃ input-number 1]]

The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=\frac{n^2+1}{n+1}$.
  
$a_7=$ [[☃ input-number 1]]

The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=6-\frac{3}{{{n}^{2}}}$.

$\displaystyle{{a}_{4}}=$ [[☃ input-number 1]]

The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=\frac{n+5}{n-3}$.
  
$a_{10}=$ [[☃ input-number 1]]

The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=\frac{n-1}{n+1}$.
  
$a_3=$ [[☃ input-number 1]]

The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=\frac{3n+2}{2n}$.
  
$a_6=$ [[☃ input-number 1]]

The $n\text{th}$ partial sum of the series $\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty }{{{a}_{n}}}$ is given by

$\displaystyle{{S}_{n}}=3-\frac{8}{n}$.
 
$a_8=$ [[☃ input-number 1]]
